_fixture_note: SAMPLE DATA FOR DEMONSTRATION ONLY — not from a production vulnerability scan.

Vulnerabilities.Title: openssl: Possible denial of service in X.509 name checks
Vulnerabilities.Description: Issue in name constraint checking in X.509 certificate path validation may allow resource exhaustion.

Vulnerabilities.Title: openssl: Use-after-free in error processing DTLS connections
Vulnerabilities.Description: A flaw in OpenSSL DTLS implementation may lead to memory corruption under specific handshake conditions.

Vulnerabilities.Title: curl: improper cleanup in websocket disconnect path
Vulnerabilities.Description: libcurl could leak credentials or crash when handling websocket teardown in certain configurations.

Vulnerabilities.Title: curl: IPv6 address parser buffer overflow
Vulnerabilities.Description: Heap buffer overflow in curl's IPv6 parsing could be triggered by a malicious URL.

Vulnerabilities.Title: openssh: slow key exchange allows CPU exhaustion
Vulnerabilities.Description: No vendor fix available in current Debian security advisory for this package series; track upstream advisory.

Vulnerabilities.Title: expat: stack exhaustion in XML entity expansion
Vulnerabilities.Description: Excessive entity expansion may cause stack exhaustion when parsing untrusted XML.

Vulnerabilities.Title: sqlite: integer overflow in window function processing
Vulnerabilities.Description: Malformed SQL could trigger undefined behavior in window function evaluation.

Vulnerabilities.Title: xz: race condition in threaded decoder
Vulnerabilities.Description: Concurrency bug may lead to incorrect decompressed output under rare timing conditions.

Vulnerabilities.Title: git: recursive clone RCE via malicious submodule configuration
Vulnerabilities.Description: Recursive clone could execute hooks from a crafted repository layout; lower impact in minimal CI images without git clone usage.

Vulnerabilities.Title: expat: parsing large tokens may allocate excessive memory
Vulnerabilities.Description: Denial of service via memory pressure when parsing very large tokens.

Vulnerabilities.Title: pam: off-by-one in pam_namespace relabel logic
Vulnerabilities.Description: Local misconfiguration edge case; low practical impact in container context.

Vulnerabilities.Title: ncurses: NULL pointer dereference in tic
Vulnerabilities.Description: Crash in tic when processing malformed terminfo sources.

Vulnerabilities.Title: glibc: advisory under analysis; severity pending
Vulnerabilities.Description: Upstream assessment in progress; treat as informational until NVD CVSS is published.

Vulnerabilities.Title: aiohttp: XSS on static file handling when follow_symlinks is enabled
Vulnerabilities.Description: Static file middleware may serve unintended paths when symbolic links are followed; severity downgraded in some environments per vendor advisory context.

Vulnerabilities.Title: werkzeug: high resource consumption when parsing multipart form data
Vulnerabilities.Description: Crafted multipart requests may cause excessive CPU usage; rated LOW for typical API-only workloads behind size limits.

Vulnerabilities.Title: flask: unexpected behavior when serving static files on Windows
Vulnerabilities.Description: Path handling edge case primarily affecting Windows deployments; low relevance for Linux images.

Vulnerabilities.Title: flask: session cookie not cleared on logout under specific config
Vulnerabilities.Description: Session fixation mitigation may be incomplete when SESSION_REFRESH_EACH_REQUEST is misconfigured.
